zotero.abstractNote: Adversarial robustness of semantic segmentation models is a critical requirement for safety-critical applications like autonomous driving. However, evaluating and training robust segmentation models is computationally expensive due to the high dimensionality of the output space. In this paper, we propose a new, efficient adversarial attack for semantic segmentation, which we call the 'Segmentation Attack' (SegAttack). SegAttack is based on a novel loss function that accounts for the spatial structure of the segmentation mask and is significantly faster than existing attacks. We further propose a fast training method for robust segmentation models, which we call 'Segmentation Adversarial Training' (SegAT). SegAT leverages the efficiency of SegAttack to train robust models in a fraction of the time required by standard adversarial training. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several standard benchmarks, showing that it achieves state-of-the-art robustness while being significantly faster to train than existing methods.
